grade_reason: Automatic F — red-teamer confirmed live breach of critical pentest objective: state machine bypass via force-approve (RED-002). Two additional confirmed Critical breaches: zero authentication (RED-001), orchestrator SSRF proxy (RED-008).

findings.title: Zero Authentication — All API Endpoints Fully Exposed
findings.description: The entire Express application has zero authentication middleware. No JWT verification, session checks, API key validation, or identity enforcement exists. The red-teamer confirmed all endpoints return HTTP 200 to unauthenticated curl requests. This is the root cause of all Critical findings.
findings.remediation: Add authentication middleware (JWT via jsonwebtoken or API key check) as the first Express middleware before all route registrations. Add RBAC to gate state-mutating endpoints (approve, reject, dispatch).

findings.title: State Machine Bypass via Force-Approve — Critical Objective ACHIEVED
findings.status: Confirmed (Live Exploit) — Critical Pentest Objective Achieved
findings.objective_achieved: Bypass work item state machine to reach an invalid status
findings.description: Red-teamer executed a 3-step unauthenticated exploit chain that approved a work item with 0 votes and bypassed the entire four-role AI assessment pod. FR-0003 confirmed 'approved' with empty votes array.
findings.remediation: 1) Implement authentication (C-001). 2) Remove or gate force-approve behind privileged role requiring completed assessment. 3) Add server-side prerequisite validation (votes cast, assessment completed) before any approval transition.

findings.title: Unauthenticated Orchestrator Proxy — SSRF and Internal API Exposure
findings.note: New finding — not in pen-tester attack surface map. Discovered against portal/Backend/; verify in Source/Backend/.
findings.description: The /api/orchestrator/* route proxies all unauthenticated requests to an internal orchestrator at http://localhost:8080. Error responses leak the internal URL. Enables unauthenticated interaction with internal infrastructure.
findings.remediation: 1) Add auth + authorization before the orchestrator proxy route. 2) Strip internal URL from error responses. 3) Allowlist permitted orchestrator API paths. 4) Consider internal-only port exposure.

findings.title: Missing HTTP Security Headers and No CORS Policy
findings.description: No security headers set: no CSP, no X-Frame-Options, no HSTS, no X-Content-Type-Options, no Referrer-Policy. No CORS policy enforced — Express default permits cross-origin requests from any origin.
findings.remediation: Install helmet as first middleware: app.use(helmet()). Add cors() with explicit origin allowlist for the frontend domain.

findings.title: Unauthenticated Prometheus Metrics Endpoint — Operational Intelligence Leak
findings.description: Red-teamer confirmed GET /metrics returns full Prometheus output with zero authentication, including route-by-route counts, Node.js heap/CPU/GC metrics, and AI voting invocation counters.
findings.remediation: Restrict /metrics to internal network or add Bearer token check. Ensure metrics port is not externally exposed in Docker/Kubernetes.

findings.title: Unbounded Pagination Limit — Full Dataset Enumeration in One Request
findings.objective_achieved: Enumerate all work items without pagination limit enforcement
findings.description: Red-teamer confirmed all 22 items returned in one unauthenticated call. ?limit=999999 silently ignored. No pagination enforced. NaN guard also missing.
findings.remediation: Cap limit: Math.min(parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20, 100). Add NaN guard: parseInt(req.query.page) || 1.

findings.title: Soft-Delete Dependency DoS — Permanent Dispatch Block
findings.description: Deleting a dependency blocker permanently blocks dependent item readiness. Root cause: no ON DELETE CASCADE on dependencies table foreign key. Confirmed by red-teamer.
findings.remediation: Add ON DELETE CASCADE to dependencies foreign key, or treat orphaned dependencies as resolved in readiness check. Gate delete behind auth for items with dependents.

findings.title: Unauthenticated Intake Webhooks — No HMAC Verification and No Enum Validation
findings.description: Intake endpoints accept arbitrary POST requests with no HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. Type and priority fields passed directly to createWorkItem without enum validation.
findings.remediation: 1) Add HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for Zendesk endpoint. 2) Add enum validation for body.type and body.priority.

findings.title: Zero Authorization / RBAC — No Least-Privilege Enforcement
findings.description: No roles, no permissions, no least-privilege model. Every authenticated user would have full access to all operations including approve, reject, dispatch, and delete. SOC2 CC6.3 and OWASP-ASVS V4 failures.
findings.remediation: Design RBAC model with read-only, operator, and admin roles. Gate approve/reject/dispatch/delete behind operator/admin. Apply checks at service layer.

findings.title: Required Audit Events Missing — Login / Permission Denied / Data Export Unlogged
findings.description: Three of four required audit events are absent: login_attempt, permission_denied, data_export. Only state_transition is logged. No actor identity in any log entry.
findings.remediation: After implementing auth: log login_attempt, permission_denied (in auth middleware), and data_export. Inject actor ID into all structured log entries.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on Any Endpoint
findings.description: No rate limiting middleware exists. Assessment and cycle-detection endpoints are unbounded CPU/memory consumers. Intake endpoints especially exposed since unauthenticated.
findings.remediation: Add express-rate-limit: app.use(rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 100 })). Stricter limits on assessment and intake routes.

findings.title: Error Message Disclosure in 500 Responses
findings.description: All six workflow action endpoints return raw err.message to clients on 500 errors, bypassing the global errorHandler. Internal paths or connection strings may be disclosed.
findings.remediation: Return generic 'Internal server error' to clients in all per-route catch blocks. Log full error server-side.

findings.title: Invalid Enum Values Accepted via Intake — Potential State Corruption
findings.description: type and priority fields from intake requests passed to createWorkItem without enum validation. Invalid values stored without error, corrupting state machine logic downstream.
findings.remediation: Add enum validation mirroring the main work items POST handler. Reject invalid values with HTTP 400.

findings.title: Stored XSS via Unescaped Text Fields
findings.description: Work item text fields stored as-is. If frontend renders them as HTML without escaping, stored XSS is possible. Severity amplified by missing CSP (H-001).
findings.remediation: Sanitize input server-side (DOMPurify/sanitize-html). Ensure frontend renders user content as text nodes. Implement CSP (H-001) as defense-in-depth.

findings.title: Docker Container Runs as Root
findings.description: No USER directive in portal Dockerfile. Node.js process runs as root. Amplifies impact of any code execution vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Add non-root user before CMD: RUN addgroup/adduser; USER appuser.

findings.title: CI Workflows Use --dangerously-skip-permissions
findings.description: All CI workflows bypass Claude Code permission prompts. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS are in scope in the same environment. A prompt-injected agent has no friction barrier.
findings.remediation: Narrow --allowedTools per agent role. Ensure secrets are not readable from Bash tool execution environment. Consider sandbox mode for sensitive pipelines.

findings.title: Unvalidated page/limit Parameters — NaN/Edge Cases
findings.description: parseInt returns NaN for non-numeric values; NaN propagates to slice logic. Negative page values not guarded. Can cause 500 errors or unexpected empty responses.
findings.remediation: page = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.page) || 1); limit = Math.min(Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20), 100).

findings.title: Predictable Sequential Document IDs — Resource Enumeration
findings.description: Sequential predictable IDs (WI-001, WI-002...) leak system load, timing, and item count. Combined with zero auth, enables trivial full enumeration.
findings.remediation: Use random UUIDs or ULIDs for docId. If sequential IDs required for UX, ensure only accessible to authenticated users.

findings.title: Debug Portal iframe Source Controllable via Build-Time Env Var
findings.description: VITE_PORTAL_URL controls iframe src. Compromised build pipeline could inject malicious origin. /debug route accessible in production builds.
findings.remediation: Validate portalUrl against allowlist at build time. Add sandbox attribute to iframe. Guard route with import.meta.env.PROD check.

findings.title: No Input Length Validation on Text Fields (Source/Backend)
findings.note: Red-teamer confirmed portal/Backend enforces length limits. Source/Backend not verified.
findings.description: No server-side input length validation on text fields in Source/Backend. portal/Backend enforces limits at service layer.
findings.remediation: Add max-length validation to all text fields in Source/Backend to match portal backend, or extract shared validation into Source/Shared/.

mitigations_confirmed_working.notes: Parameterized queries used consistently — not exploitable
Service layer enforces title ≤ 200, description ≤ 10,000 chars
Transition validator blocks invalid jumps (potential → completed)

red_teamer.note: Tested portal/Backend/ (target mismatch vs pen-tester's Source/Backend/)
